name: Shrimp Creole
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Fresh basil or 2 teaspoons dried basil
1 tablespoon Fresh thyme or 2 teaspoons dried thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and devein the shrimp.
2. Place the heads (if available) and peels in a small saucepan with water. Bring to a slow boil over medium-high heat and let boil slowly for 15-20 minutes. Strain and discard the heads and peels.
3. Heat the oil in a Dutch oven or other large, heavy pot over medium-high heat. Add the onions, bell peppers, and celery. Stir often until the vegetables are very soft, about 45 minutes.
4. Stir in the tomatoes, salt, red pepper, white pepper, herbs, sugar, and shrimp stock. Return to simmer.
5. Reduce heat to medium and let simmer for 2 hours, stirring occasionally. The sauce can be prepared up to 2 days in advance and stored in the refrigerator (the sauce is even better after sitting a couple of days).
6. When ready to serve, return the sauce to a simmer and add the shrimp. Cook until they turn pink, about 5-7 minutes.
7. Stir in the green onions and parsley and cook for 1 minute more. Serve over beds of rice on flat plates. Serves 6-8.
Shrimp and Ham or Tasso Jambalaya:
Prepare Shrimp Creole as above, but add about a pound of cubed ham and a 4-ounce can of tomato sauce to the finished sauce. Simmer for an additional 45 minutes. Meanwhile, boil or steam 2-3 cups raw rice. Finish the sauce (which will be your jambalaya base) by adding the shrimp, green onions, and parsley as above. If using leftover Shrimp Creole, remove the shrimp and reheat the sauce. Add the ham and tomato sauce, then proceed as above. Return the shrimp to the pan at the end of cooking. Green onions and parsley are stirred in for 1 minute more before serving over rice. Serves 6-8.
You can also prepare this dish with sausage instead of ham.
